title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon)
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) at home! This authentic recipe features fresh seafood and a crispy texture. Get the full recipe now!
longTailKeywords: how to make Korean seafood pancake
Haemul Pajeon step by step instructions
seo.h1: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?
Pro Tips for the Crispiest Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.imageAlt: A freshly made, crispy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) with dipping sauce
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Korea with this delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon, packed with fresh seafood and vibrant flavors.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved crispy Korean savory pancake packed with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This classic dish offers a delightful combination of chewy seafood, tender scallions, and a perfectly golden-brown, crisp exterior, making it an irresistible appetizer or light meal. It is traditionally served hot with a tangy soy-vinegar dipping sauce, creating a harmonious balance of flavors and textures.

ingredients.notes: peeled, deveined, cut in half if large

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Pat shrimp and squid dry with paper towels. If using large shrimp, cut them in half. Cut green onions into 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the onion and red chili if using. Ensure all ingredients are ready before mixing the batter.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, egg, and cold water (or broth) until just combined. A few lumps are okay; do not overmix. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to a traditional pancake batter consistency.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, minced garlic, and red pepper flakes. Mix well until all ingredients are thoroughly blended. Set the dipping sauce aside, ready to serve with your pancake.

instructions.title: Cook the First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Pour about 1/3 to 1/2 of the batter into the hot pan, spreading it evenly with the back of a ladle to form a thin, round layer.

instructions.title: Add Toppings and Flip
instructions.text: Quickly arrange a portion of the green onions, sliced onion, shrimp, squid, and red chili evenly over the batter. Drizzle a little more batter over the toppings if needed to help them stick. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking and Serve
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges and cook for another 4-5 minutes, pressing down gently with a spatula, until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. Transfer to a cutting board, slice into wedges, and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Repeat for remaining batter and toppings.

tips: For extra crispiness, use very cold water or chilled anchovy broth in your batter. This helps create a lighter, crispier texture when fried.
Do not overmix the batter; a few lumps are fine. Overmixing develops the gluten, resulting in a chewier rather than crispy pancake.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking. Its best enjoyed hot and crispy. If making multiple, keep earlier pancakes warm in a low oven.
article:
Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean seafood pancake, is a culinary masterpiece that tantalizes taste buds with its crispy edges, chewy interior, and abundance of fresh seafood and aromatic scallions. More than just a simple pancake, it’s a celebration of textures and flavors, a staple at Korean restaurants, street food stalls, and family tables across the globe. Whether you're a seasoned Korean food enthusiast or new to its vibrant cuisine, mastering Haemul Pajeon is a rewarding experience that brings a piece of Korea right into your kitchen.

This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about making the perfect Korean Seafood Pancake, from understanding its essence to crafting the crispiest, most delicious version. Get ready to impress your family and friends with this savory delight!

## What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?
Haemul Pajeon literally translates to "seafood scallion pancake." "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pajeon" refers to a scallion pancake. Unlike sweet breakfast pancakes, Pajeon is a savory, pan-fried dish often enjoyed as an appetizer, a side dish (banchan), or a hearty snack. Its distinct characteristic lies in its unique texture – a delicate balance of a golden-brown, crispy exterior achieved through pan-frying, and a soft, slightly chewy interior packed with a medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green scallions.

The beauty of Haemul Pajeon lies in its versatility and comforting appeal. It's a dish that embodies the spirit of Korean communal dining, often shared among friends over a glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or soju. The generous inclusion of seafood like shrimp, squid, mussels, and sometimes even oysters, combined with the refreshing bite of scallions, makes this Korean Seafood Pancake a truly unforgettable experience.

## Ingredients You'll Need
Creating an authentic Haemul Pajeon requires a careful selection of ingredients to ensure the perfect texture and flavor profile.

**For the Pancake Batter:**
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/4 cup potato starch or corn starch (crucial for crispiness!)
*   1 large egg
*   1 1/2 cups very cold water or anchovy broth (cold liquid helps with crispiness)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper

**For the Filling:**
*   2 bunches green onions (scallions), about 15-20 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch lengths
*   1 pound mixed seafood (shrimp, squid rings, mussels, bay scallops, or a pre-made seafood mix), thawed if frozen, cleaned and roughly chopped into bite-sized pieces
*   1/2 small onion, thinly sliced (optional, for extra flavor)
*   1 small red or green chili, thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)
*   2-3 tablespoons vegetable oil (for frying, more may be needed)

**For the Dipping Sauce (Yangnyeom Ganjang):**
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sugar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)
*   1 clove garlic, minced
*   1 tablespoon finely chopped green onion

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Haemul Pajeon
Follow these steps carefully to achieve a restaurant-quality Korean Seafood Pancake at home.

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Scallions:** If using frozen seafood, ensure it's fully thawed and patted dry to prevent a watery batter. Clean and chop all seafood into bite-sized pieces. Wash the scallions thoroughly and trim off the root ends. Cut them into 3-4 inch lengths. If using, slice the onion and chili.

2.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, potato starch (or corn starch), salt, and black pepper. In a separate small bowl, whisk the egg with the cold water or broth. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until just combined. A few lumps are perfectly fine; do not overmix, as this can lead to a tough pancake.

3.  **Combine Filling with Batter:** Gently fold the prepared seafood, sliced onion (if using), and most of the scallions into the batter. Reserve a handful of the longer scallion pieces for arranging on top of the pancake during cooking.

4.  **Heat the Pan:** Heat a large (10-12 inch) non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan. The oil should be shimmering hot before you add the batter.

5.  **Cook the Pancake:** Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin, round pancake. Immediately arrange some of the reserved long scallion pieces on top of the batter in an attractive pattern. You can also press a few more seafood pieces onto the top if desired.

6.  **Fry until Golden:** Cook for 4-6 minutes on the first side, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges start to look set. Don't be afraid to lift an edge gently to check the color.

7.  **Flip and Finish:** Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. You might need to add another tablespoon of oil around the edges to help the second side crisp up. Cook for another 3-5 minutes on the second side, until golden brown and cooked through. Repeat with the remaining batter.

8.  **Prepare Dipping Sauce:** While the pancakes are cooking, combine all the dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl and stir well.

## Pro Tips for the Crispiest Korean Seafood Pancake
Achieving that coveted crispiness is key to a truly outstanding Haemul Pajeon. Here are some expert tips:

*   **Cold Batter is Your Friend:** Using very cold water or broth for your batter helps inhibit gluten development, resulting in a lighter, crisper pancake. You can even chill your flour beforehand.
*   **Don't Skip the Starch:** Potato or corn starch is essential. It adds an incredible crispness that flour alone cannot provide.
*   **Generous Oil is Key:** Don't skimp on the oil! Haemul Pajeon is pan-fried, not just lightly cooked. Enough oil ensures a golden, crispy crust.
*   **Hot Pan, Medium-High Heat:** Start with a very hot pan to get an immediate sizzle. Maintain medium-high heat to cook through without burning.
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** Cook one pancake at a time in a large skillet. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and steams the pancake instead of frying it.
*   **Thin is In:** Aim for a relatively thin pancake. Thicker pancakes tend to be doughier and less crispy.
*   **Press Down Lightly:** After flipping, gently press down on the pancake with your spatula to ensure even contact with the hot pan and promote browning.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce
Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed immediately, straight from the pan, while it's still hot and crispy. Cut it into wedges or squares using a pizza cutter or knife.

*   **Pairings:** It’s traditionally served with a simple yet flavorful dipping sauce (Yangnyeom Ganjang) which cuts through the richness of the pancake. For an authentic Korean experience, pair your Korean Seafood Pancake with a bottle of chilled makgeolli or soju. It also makes an excellent appetizer before a larger Korean meal, or a fantastic standalone lunch.
*   **Dipping Sauce:** The dipping sauce is crucial for balancing the savory flavors of the pancake. The recipe provided above offers a perfect blend of salty, tangy, and slightly sweet, with a hint of spice. Feel free to adjust the amount of gochugaru to your spice preference.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a delight, don't hesitate to experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan Pajeon:** Omit the seafood and egg. Increase the amount of scallions and add other vegetables like thinly sliced mushrooms (oyster, shiitake), zucchini, carrots, or even kimchi (for Kimchi Pajeon). For a vegan version, use a flax egg or a commercial egg replacer for the batter.
*   **Different Seafood:** Customize your seafood mix based on what's fresh and available. Clams, oysters, or even chunks of white fish can be excellent additions.
*   **Add More Vegetables:** Beyond scallions, feel free to incorporate other finely julienned vegetables such as bell peppers, chives, or even a handful of bean sprouts for added crunch.
*   **Spicier Kick:** Add a teaspoon of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) directly into the batter for an all-over spicy flavor, or include more sliced fresh chilies.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Substitute the all-purpose flour with a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend. Ensure your chosen blend contains xanthan gum or add a small amount to help with binding.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's an experience that brings people together. Its irresistible combination of crispy texture, fresh seafood, and aromatic scallions makes it a standout in Korean cuisine. By following these tips and embracing the joy of cooking, you'll be able to create a memorable Korean Seafood Pancake that will have everyone asking for seconds. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and get ready to fry up a piece of Korean culinary magic!
